name: Chicago
localTips: Get a Ventra card or just tap a contactless card on the CTA — the Blue Line runs 24/7 from O'Hare to the Loop for the price of a regular fare.
In winter, learn the Pedway: 40 blocks of heated underground and skybridge corridors connect Loop stations, Macy's and the Cultural Center without going outside.
The lake makes its own weather — it can be 10 degrees colder and twice as windy on the lakefront trail than three blocks inland, any month of the year.
Locals mostly eat thin 'tavern-cut' pizza; deep dish is a once-a-trip event, not a lunch. Order a Chicago dog with everything — just never ask for ketchup.
Tipping runs 20% at restaurants and $1–2 per drink at bars; many spots add a service charge for groups, so check the bill before doubling up.

activities.title: Chicago Riverwalk at dusk
activities.description: A mile and a half of terraced promenade below street level — wine kiosks, kayaks and the corncob towers of Marina City glowing over the green river.

activities.title: Millennium Park & Cloud Gate
activities.description: The Bean's funhouse skyline reflections, Crown Fountain's spitting video faces, and Gehry's ribboned Pritzker Pavilion — Chicago's front lawn in one loop.

activities.title: Chicago Cultural Center's Tiffany dome
activities.description: The world's largest Tiffany glass dome crowns this free palace of mosaics and marble opposite Millennium Park — rotating art exhibitions cost nothing either.

activities.title: Garfield Park Conservatory
activities.description: Two acres of jungle, ferns and desert under glass on the West Side — 'landscape art under glass' since 1908, and gloriously tropical when it's sleeting outside.

activities.title: Lincoln Park Zoo
activities.description: One of America's last free zoos — lions, gorillas and a skyline backdrop, with the Nature Boardwalk pond framing the Hancock through prairie grass.

activities.title: Lakefront Trail by bike or on foot
activities.description: Eighteen car-free miles along Lake Michigan — skyline at Museum Campus, beach volleyball at North Avenue, and the whole city rising out of the water.

activities.title: Navy Pier & the Centennial Wheel
activities.description: Free to wander: the half-mile pier with skyline views, street performers and summer fireworks — the enclosed-gondola Ferris wheel runs year-round if you want the ride.

activities.title: Loop public art & architecture walk
activities.description: The untitled Picasso at Daley Plaza, Chagall's Four Seasons mosaic, Calder's Flamingo and Miró's Sun — a world-class sculpture garden scattered under the L tracks.

activities.title: Pilsen murals & National Museum of Mexican Art
activities.description: Block after block of murals along 16th and 18th Streets, then the free National Museum of Mexican Art — one of the country's best collections, especially around Day of the Dead.

activities.title: The 606 elevated trail
activities.description: A 2.7-mile rail line turned elevated park threading Wicker Park, Bucktown and Humboldt Park — Chicago's answer to the High Line, minus the crowds.

activities.title: North Avenue Beach skyline swim
activities.description: Sand, volleyball courts and the boat-shaped beach house with the Hancock tower looming behind — the classic Chicago-summer postcard, free as the lake.

activities.title: Wicker Park record shops & Milwaukee Avenue
activities.description: Crate-digging at Reckless Records, shelves at Myopic Books and coffee at intersections that launched a thousand bands — the North Side's indie main street.

activities.title: Grant Park & Buckingham Fountain
activities.description: One of the world's largest fountains firing its 150-foot center jet on the hour (April–October), with Museum Campus and the lake beyond — evening light shows too.

activities.title: Jazz at the Green Mill
activities.description: Al Capone's favorite booth still faces both doors at this 1907 Uptown lounge — velvet, neon and serious jazz nightly, including a 4am license on weekends.
activities.insiderNote: Cash only, $5–15 cover. Capone's booth is the one at the short end of the bar; Sunday night's Uptown Poetry Slam is the longest-running in the world.

activities.title: Italian beef, dipped, standing up
activities.description: Thin-sliced roast beef on gravy-soaked bread with hot giardiniera — eaten in the 'Italian stance' leaning over the counter at Johnnie's or Al's #1.
activities.insiderNote: Order it 'hot, dipped' — the whole sandwich dunked in jus. Johnnie's Beef in Elmwood Park is the purist pick; Al's on Taylor Street is the historic one. Cash at Johnnie's.

activities.title: The Rookery's Frank Lloyd Wright lobby
activities.description: Inside Burnham & Root's 1888 masterpiece on LaSalle Street hides a white-marble-and-gold light court remodeled by Frank Lloyd Wright in 1905 — free to walk into.
activities.insiderNote: The lobby is open weekday business hours — just walk in past security. The oriel staircase from the second floor is the shot everyone misses.

activities.title: Caramelized-crust pan pizza at Pequod's
activities.description: The locals' deep-dish answer: pan pizza with a ring of blackened, caramelized cheese crust, served in a Lincoln Park tavern that stays loud till late.
activities.insiderNote: Call ahead and pre-order your pizza while you wait for a table — it saves the 45-minute bake. The half-sausage, half-pepperoni settles all arguments.

activities.title: Billy Goat Tavern under Michigan Avenue
activities.description: Down the stairs to Lower Michigan Avenue: the 1934 newspapermen's dive that inspired SNL's 'cheezborger, cheezborger' sketch and the Cubs' billy goat curse.
activities.insiderNote: It's on the hidden lower level of Michigan Avenue — take the stairs by the Wrigley Building. Say 'double cheezborger'; they'll say 'no fries, cheeps' — that's the ritual.

activities.title: Late-night blues at Kingston Mines
activities.description: Two stages alternating live Chicago blues until 2:30am (later Saturdays) in a Lincoln Park institution going since 1968 — sweaty, loud, exactly right.
activities.insiderNote: Bands alternate stages all night, so move rooms between sets. B.L.U.E.S. across the street is smaller and grittier — one cover often gets bar-hoppers into both scenes.

activities.title: Devon Avenue South Asian food crawl
activities.description: A mile of Little India and Pakistan on the far North Side — nihari at Sabri Nihari, chaat counters, sari shops and sweets by the boxload.
activities.insiderNote: Come hungry on a weekend afternoon: order frontier chicken at Sabri Nihari and finish with kulfi from a sweets counter. The Red Line to Loyola plus bus 155 gets you there.

activities.title: Promontory Point at sunrise
activities.description: A man-made limestone peninsula in Hyde Park with the entire skyline stacked across the water — locals' favorite swim spot off the rocks in summer.
activities.insiderNote: The stepped limestone revetment is where Hyde Parkers actually swim — no lifeguards, know your limits. Sunrise here beats any observation deck, and you'll share it with a handful of joggers.

activities.title: Garrett Mix from the original popcorn shops
activities.description: The cheddar-and-caramel 'Garrett Mix' has perfumed downtown street corners since 1949 — a warm bag eaten walking the Mag Mile is a Chicago rite.
activities.insiderNote: Locals never say 'Chicago Mix' — it's Garrett Mix. Get the small unless you want a commitment, and eat it same-day while the caramel still snaps.

activities.title: Art Institute of Chicago
activities.description: Behind the bronze lions: Seurat's Grande Jatte, Hopper's Nighthawks, American Gothic and the Thorne Miniature Rooms — a top-five museum on any continent.
activities.bookingQuery: Art Institute of Chicago skip the line ticket

activities.title: Architecture river cruise
activities.description: Ninety minutes on the river with a docent decoding the skyline building by building, from the Wrigley Building to Aqua's rippling balconies — the city's best-loved tour for a reason.

activities.title: Skydeck Ledge at Willis Tower
activities.description: Step onto a glass box hanging 1,353 feet over Wacker Drive — on a clear day you can see four states from the 103rd floor.
activities.bookingQuery: Willis Tower Skydeck Ledge ticket Chicago

activities.title: 360 CHICAGO & TILT
activities.description: The old Hancock observatory tilts you face-first over Michigan Avenue from the 94th floor — the lake-and-skyline view here beats the Skydeck at sunset.

activities.title: Field Museum & SUE the T. rex
activities.description: SUE, the most complete T. rex ever found, plus mummies and man-eating lions of Tsavo in a Beaux-Arts hall on Museum Campus.

activities.title: Museum of Science and Industry
activities.description: A captured German U-505 submarine, a full coal mine, and a 727 hanging in the hall — the vast 1893 World's Fair palace in Hyde Park earns a whole day.
activities.bookingQuery: Museum of Science and Industry Chicago ticket

activities.title: Shedd Aquarium
activities.description: Belugas, sea otters and a Caribbean reef in a 1930 marble octagon on the lakefront — book timed entry; the Oceanarium's amphitheater looks straight out at the lake.
activities.bookingQuery: Shedd Aquarium Chicago timed entry ticket

activities.title: The Second City mainstage
activities.description: The sketch-and-improv theater that trained Belushi, Murray, Fey and Colbert — the mainstage revue in Old Town is still where comedy's next names are working.

activities.title: Chicago Magic Lounge
activities.description: Enter through a fake laundromat into an Art Deco cabaret where close-up magicians work every table before the main stage show — Chicago-style magic reborn.

activities.title: Cubs game at Wrigley Field
activities.description: Ivy on the outfield walls, a hand-turned scoreboard and 'Go Cubs Go' after a win — baseball in its 1914 cathedral, bleacher ticket in hand.
